Tests the conversion of a non-empty string to a Boolean true value.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the truthiness of a non-empty string in JavaScript. The expression `Boolean("abcdef")` converts the string `"abcdef"` to a boolean value, which is `true` because non-empty strings are truthy in JavaScript. The `assert` function then verifies that this boolean value is indeed `true`. This code provides narrow functionality, serving as a basic test to confirm the behavior of the `Boolean` function with non-empty strings.
